article_title: Astronomers Directly Observe the Rotation of a Planet-Forming Disk for the First Time, Tracking AB Aurigae Over Four Years
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

findings.message: Source redirected to different domain: aanda.org
Near-verbatim quote: the Boccaletti title quote omits the word 'the'
findings.details: The article quotes Observatoire de Paris describing Boccaletti as a 'CNRS Research Director at Paris Observatory - PSL (LIRA)'. The source reads 'CNRS Research Director at the Paris Observatory - PSL (LIRA)' — the word 'the' was dropped. Meaning is unchanged; filed as a clarification.

content_preview.title: Astronomers Directly Observe the Rotation of a Planet-Forming Disk for the First Time, Tracking AB Aurigae Over Four Years
content_preview.summary: A CNRS-led team used the VLT's SPHERE instrument to watch a protoplanetary disk spin, finding anomalies linked to forming giant planets.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Astronomers have directly observed the rotation of a protoplanetary disk for the first time, watching the cloud of dust and gas that surrounds the young star AB Aurigae turn over a four-year span. According to a press release from the [CNRS](https://www.cnrs.fr/en/press/first-ever-live-observation-rotation-planetary-nursery), "the rotation of a protoplanetary disc (a disc where planets are being formed) has been observed directly for the very first time by mapping the emissions from...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
1 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically

editor_notes.content_quality: Clear, well-sourced astronomy News piece (702 words) that distinguishes the directly-observed rotation result from the inferred forming-planet interpretation and is carefully hedged.
editor_notes.source_verification: Five of six snapshots read from disk (HTTP 200); Phys.org (source-2) returned 403 and the DOI (source-5) resolved to aanda.org (Astronomy & Astrophysics). CNRS (s0) confirms verbatim the 'first ever' direct-rotation quote, the anomaly quote ('certain regions close to the star show an unexpected departure …'), and the 'a body of evidence suggests … giant planets in the process of formation' quote. Observatoire de Paris (s1) confirms the accretion-structure and shadow/protoplanet quotes AND carries verbatim the SPHERE-instrument quote that the article attributes to Phys.org ('Thanks to the unique near-infrared capabilities of the SPHERE instrument and its exceptional spatial resolution …'); the University-of-Bordeaux co-leadership and the A&A publication are corroborated here, in CNRS, and in Universe Today. ESO (s4) confirms all three image-caption quotes. Universe Today (s3) confirms AB Aurigae's '4 or 5 million years' age and the AB Aurigae b '93 astronomical units' / '9 Jupiter masses' figures plus the 30 AU and 400-600 AU candidate bodies. The DOI/aanda.org page (s5) confirms the paper title 'Destructuring the disk of AB Aurigae: Dynamics and accretion' and Boccaletti as first author. The Phys.org 403 is non-blocking: both claims attributed to it (the CNRS/Bordeaux + A&A lead and the SPHERE quote) are independently verified in reachable cited sources.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every figure, name, and quote is accurate and traceable. Two minor issues: (1) the SPHERE-instrument quote is attributed to Phys.org (bot-blocked, 403) but is verbatim in the cited, reachable Observatoire de Paris release — the content is solid regardless of which outlet is credited; (2) the quote-marked Boccaletti title drops the word 'the' ('at Paris Observatory' vs the source's 'at the Paris Observatory'). Neither changes meaning; filed as clarifications.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral; 'What We Don't Know' appropriately frames the forming-planet interpretation as a body of evidence rather than a confirmed detection.
editor_notes.originality: New topic — no prior AB Aurigae disk-rotation coverage; distinct from the recently-published Kamo'oalewa/Tianwen-2 space story. The contributor avoided a stale 2020 'twist' article about the same star.
concerns: Phys.org snapshot bot-blocked (403); its two attributed claims are independently verified in the reachable CNRS, Observatoire de Paris, and Universe Today snapshots.
The SPHERE quote credited to Phys.org is verbatim in the Observatoire de Paris release; the Boccaletti title quote omits 'the'. Filed as a corrections record.
recommendations: Attribute the SPHERE-instrument quote to the Observatoire de Paris / CNRS release (where it is archived) rather than the bot-blocked Phys.org, and restore 'the' in the Boccaletti title quote.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Substantively accurate, fully-corroborated astronomy News piece with two trivial quote/attribution slips. APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS.
